reasoning: The question is whether Trump/Elon will cut >250,000 federal employees by Jan 2026 relative to Jan 2025, using FRED data. The BLS reported an 84k drop in federal government employment by July 2025. However, this count may not catch the full backlog, and news reports reveal the scope is vastly larger. Reuters reported in late April that 260,000 civil servants had ALREADY exited through firings, resignations, and early retirements. More importantly, after a May injunction temporarily halted layoffs, the Supreme Court reversed it in July 2025, clearing the way for mass cuts at massive agencies like DoD and DHS. Furthermore, many employees were put on administrative leave or given notice months in advance (e.g., NPR noted leave ending June 9), meaning they drop off official BLS payrolls later in the year than when they were announced or ceased working. The momentum indicates federal cuts are absolutely targeting far above the 250k mark. Since 260k exits are reported to have happened by late April and the SCOTUS opened the floodgates again in July, the final January 2026 data point on FRED should reflect more than a 250k decline from Jan 2025. I am placing my probability at 75%.
